title: Desmos zeylanicus
uses: Desmos zeylanicus, also known as the Ceylonese Desmos, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits. intro: Desmos zeylanicus, also known as the Indian gentian or gentian of Ceylon, is a flowering plant species belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Desmos zeylanicus include gentiopicroside, gentiacauleine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Desmos zeylanicus is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used to make herbal teas and infusions that are valued for their potential health benefits.
